questions.question: Explain any five services provided by an Operating System to its users.
keyPoints: User Interface (CLI/GUI/Batch)
Program Execution
I/O Operations
File System Manipulation
Communication/Error Detection/Resource Allocation/Protection/Accounting (any three)

questions.question: Differentiate between CLI and GUI with examples.
keyPoints: CLI: Text-based commands, examples - DOS, Unix shell; GUI: Visual elements, examples - Windows, macOS; GUI is more user-friendly
CLI is keyboard-driven, GUI uses mouse/pointers; GUI provides icons, windows, menus
CLI requires command knowledge, GUI is intuitive

questions.question: Describe the Error Detection and Recovery services of an OS.
keyPoints: Monitors hardware/software errors in CPU, memory, I/O devices
Uses interrupts, traps, exception handlers for detection
Takes appropriate action like termination, retry, or notification
Ensures system stability and data integrity

questions.question: What is the need for Resource Allocation service in a multiprogramming environment?
keyPoints: Multiple processes compete for limited resources
OS must allocate CPU, memory, I/O devices efficiently
Prevents deadlock and starvation
Maximizes throughput and resource utilization

questions.question: Explain Protection and Security services of an OS with examples.
keyPoints: Protection: Controls access to resources using permissions
Security: Prevents unauthorized access from external sources
Examples: Passwords, file permissions, encryption, firewalls
Prevents data theft, modification, or destruction

questions.question: Describe the Accounting service of an Operating System.
keyPoints: Tracks resource usage by users and processes
Records CPU time, memory used, I/O operations
Used for billing, performance monitoring, capacity planning
Helps in system optimization and user charge calculation

questions.question: Explain the Communication service in Operating Systems.
keyPoints: Enables processes to exchange information
Two methods: Shared Memory and Message Passing
Used for coordination between processes
Implemented through system calls like send(), receive()

questions.question: Describe in detail the major services provided by an Operating System to users and application programs.
keyPoints: User Interface: CLI, GUI, Batch processing - interaction methods
Program Execution: Loading, running, terminating programs
I/O Operations: Device-independent access to I/O devices
File System Manipulation: Create, delete, read, write files and directories
Communication: Inter-process communication via shared memory/message passing
Error Detection: Monitoring hardware/software errors
Resource Allocation: Managing CPU, memory, I/O among processes
Protection: Access control to resources
Accounting: Tracking resource usage for billing

questions.question: Explain the Process Management and Memory Management services of an Operating System.
keyPoints: Process Management: Process creation, scheduling, termination, PCB maintenance
CPU scheduling algorithms: FCFS, SJF, Round Robin
Memory Management: Memory allocation and deallocation
Virtual memory, paging, segmentation
Address translation and protection
Memory allocation strategies: Fixed, variable, paging

questions.question: Discuss the file system services provided by an Operating System. How does the OS ensure data security?
keyPoints: File operations: Create, delete, read, write, open, close
Directory operations: Create, delete, navigate
File permissions and access control
Protection through passwords, encryption
User authentication
Operating system security mechanisms: Firewalls, antivirus

questions.question: Explain the role of Operating System as a Resource Manager with special reference to CPU, Memory, and I/O management.
keyPoints: OS manages CPU through scheduling algorithms
Memory management: allocation, deallocation, virtual memory
I/O management: device drivers, buffering, caching
Resource allocation prevents conflicts
Maximizes utilization and throughput
Handles deadlocks and resource starvation
